message:
fix(freeze): handle parameter assignment when argc < param_count

When a function is called with fewer arguments than declared parameters,
assignments to those parameters were being lost. This is because put_arg/
set_arg operations wrote to argv[N] only when argc > N, but the value
was simply discarded when argc <= N.

The fix introduces arg_shadow storage for functions that reassign params:
- When has_put_arg is true, generate arg_shadow array at function start
- Initialize from argv if provided, else UNDEFINED
- get_arg reads from arg_shadow instead of argv
- put_arg/set_arg write to arg_shadow unconditionally

This fixes RxJS's ReplaySubject and similar patterns where constructor
parameters are reassigned from closure-captured values when called
with fewer args (e.g., new ReplaySubject(2) without all 3 params).
